rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Monetary Sanctions And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 2. PLAINTIFF RIMMA BOROKHOV and DAVID TSE and MICHAEL TO's Motion For Monetary Sanctions And Attorneys' Fees DENIED. CCP 2023.030 is not applicable under the circumstances. Authorities cited in support of Plaintiff's request do not support Plaintiff's broad interpretation of Discovery Act to include "breach of oral cost splitting contract" into definition of "misuse of discovery." Pak's request for sanctions is denied. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: TELMATE,LLC VS. THE FRONT STREET BUILDING, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANT THE FRONT STREET BUILDING, LLC and RICK HOLMAN's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to September 20, 2013 on Court's own motion. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY D. MURPHY VS. MICHAEL YAKOVLEVICH KLESTOFF et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT MARTHA DAETWYLER's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED as follows: anti-SLAPP is moot as to the 4th, 5th, 6th, 7th, and 8th causes of action in light of this Court's ruling sustaining Mr. Klestoff's demurer as to those causes of action in the First Amended Complaint without leave to amend pursuant to Harris v. Wachovia (2010) 185 Cla.App.4th 1018. Defendant's motion is otherwise GRANTED. Moving party sustained its burden under the first prong of CCP 425.16 and no evidence was provided to the Court conclusively showing that Ms. Daetwyler's activities were illegal as a matter of law. Flately. Plaintiff failed to show by admissible evidence that his causes of action 1, 2, 3 and 9 have merit. Plaintiff's Request for Statement of Decision is DENIED, CCP 632 is inapplicable to this hearing. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY D. MURPHY VS. MICHAEL YAKOVLEVICH KLESTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complt
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 4. PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY MURPHY's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint DENIED in part, and supplemental briefing is required. Motion is DENIED to the extent Plaintiff seeks to re-allege causes of action to which demurrer was sustained without leave to amend. Motion is also DENIED as to any allegations regarding Defendant Daetwyler in light of ruling on Special Motion to Strike. Plaintiff is ordered to file and serve by personal or electronic service no later than September 17, 2013 a supplemental brief and declaration (total pages, excluding exhibits, not to exceed 15) specifying all remaining proposed amendments/supplemental allegations, if any. Plaintiff is also ordered to address the propriety of supplementing First Amended Complaint where the Second Amended Complaint has been filed and is the operative Complaint. Defendants shall file and serve their supplemental response (not to exceed 15 pages) no later than September 23, 2013. Hearing is set for September 25, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 501. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: JINETTA SCOTT, AS TRUSTEE OF THE CLAUDE T. SMITH, VS. JOANN M. MAHER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT JOANN MAHER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained WITH 10-Day LEAVE TO AMEND. As to the first and second causes of action, Plaintiff shall allege all elements of each cause of action in light of judicially noticeable documents and Civil Code 1066, 1105, 1636 and 1639. Request for Judicial Notice is Granted as to Defendant's Exhibits A, B and C; DENIED as to Exhibits D and E. As to the third cause of action Plaintiff shall specify which instrument she seeks to cancel. See FAC 42 versus Opposition 6:22-23. As to the fifth cause of action Plaintiff shall re-allege it as a remedy. Plaintiff's request for leave to allege a cause of action for partition is GRANTED. Moving party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: RINA BELLO VS. JAVIER TENORIO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 6. DEFENDANT JAVIER TENORIO, MARIETTA TENORIO and HARVEY SALAZAR DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Cause of action for unlawful detainer is sufficiently alleged. The actual number of pages attached controls and one page discrepancy does not render Fourth Amended Complaint unintelligible. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 12, 2013, line 7.
